name: Circular Economy Metrics and Indicators
description: Reference framework for circular economy measurement, including the Material Circularity Indicator (Ellen MacArthur Foundation), waste hierarchy metrics, product lifecycle indicators, and the EU Circular Economy Monitoring Framework.

materialCircularityIndicator.description: The Material Circularity Indicator (MCI) measures how restorative the material flows of a product or company are. It ranges from 0 (fully linear) to 1 (fully circular), accounting for the proportion of recycled/reused input, the destination of outputs (recycling, reuse, disposal), product utility (lifespan and intensity of use), and the efficiency of recycling processes.

formulaComponents.name: Virgin material input fraction
formulaComponents.description: Mass of virgin material input, where M is total mass, Fr is fraction of feedstock from recycled sources, and Fu is fraction from reused sources.

formulaComponents.name: Unrecoverable waste
formulaComponents.description: Mass of material ending up as waste (landfill/incineration without energy recovery), where Cr is fraction collected for recycling, Cu is fraction going to reuse, and Ef is efficiency of the recycling process.

formulaComponents.name: Linear Flow Index
formulaComponents.description: Measures the proportion of material flowing linearly through the system. LFI = 0 means fully circular, LFI = 1 means fully linear.

formulaComponents.name: Utility factor
formulaComponents.formula: F(X) = 0.9 / X, where X = (L / Lav) * (U / Uav)
formulaComponents.description: Accounts for product utility compared to industry average. L = actual product lifetime, Lav = industry average lifetime, U = actual functional units of use (intensity), Uav = industry average functional units. A longer-lived, more intensively used product scores higher.
formulaComponents.range: 0.9/X approaches 0 for very long-lived intensive products

formulaComponents.name: Material Circularity Indicator
formulaComponents.description: The final MCI score. Combines linear flow with utility correction. Higher values indicate more circular material flows.

scoringInterpretation.interpretation: Highly linear. Predominantly virgin inputs and waste outputs. Little to no recycled content or end-of-life recovery.
Mostly linear with some circular elements. Limited recycled content or some end-of-life recycling.
Moderate circularity. Meaningful recycled inputs and/or end-of-life recovery. Room for improvement.
Good circularity. High recycled content, effective end-of-life recovery, and/or extended product life.
Excellent circularity. Predominantly recycled/reused inputs, high end-of-life recovery, and extended utility.

applicationLevels.description: Assess material flows at a specific site

wasteHierarchyMetrics.description: Metrics aligned with the EU Waste Hierarchy (Directive 2008/98/EC, Article 4), which establishes a priority order for waste management: prevention > reuse > recycling > other recovery > disposal.

metrics.name: Waste Prevention Rate
metrics.formula: 1 - (Total waste generated / Revenue or production output)
metrics.unit: Intensity ratio or % reduction from baseline year
metrics.description: Measures the decoupling of waste generation from economic output. Tracks year-on-year reduction in waste intensity.

metrics.name: Reuse Rate
metrics.formula: Mass of materials/products reused / Total mass of materials/products
metrics.description: Proportion of materials or products that are reused for the same or different purpose without significant reprocessing.

metrics.name: Recycling Rate
metrics.formula: Mass of waste recycled / Total waste generated
metrics.description: Proportion of waste that is recycled into secondary raw materials. Includes mechanical and chemical recycling, composting, and anaerobic digestion of bio-waste.
dataRequirements: Mass of waste sent to recycling (tonnes)

metrics.name: Recovery Rate (other than recycling)
metrics.formula: Mass of waste sent to energy recovery / Total waste generated
metrics.description: Proportion of waste processed through incineration with energy recovery or other recovery operations (backfilling, etc.).
dataRequirements: Mass of waste to energy recovery (tonnes)

metrics.name: Disposal Rate (landfill + incineration without recovery)
metrics.formula: Mass of waste disposed / Total waste generated
metrics.description: Proportion of waste sent to landfill or incinerated without energy recovery. Lower is better.
dataRequirements: Mass of waste to incineration without energy recovery (tonnes)

metrics.name: Waste Diversion Rate
metrics.formula: (Total waste - Waste to landfill and incineration without recovery) / Total waste
metrics.description: Overall rate of waste diverted from disposal (landfill and incineration without energy recovery). Combines reuse, recycling, and recovery.

productLifecycleIndicators.description: Indicators measuring the circularity of products across their lifecycle, from design through end-of-life.

indicators.name: Design for Circularity Score
indicators.description: Composite score assessing how well a product is designed for circularity, covering disassembly, repairability, modularity, material selection, and end-of-life pathways.

components.name: Disassembly ease
components.description: Ability to disassemble product into components using standard tools. Scored 0-10.

components.name: Repairability
components.description: Availability of spare parts, repair documentation, and ease of common repairs. Aligned with EU repairability scoring.

components.name: Modularity
components.description: Use of modular design allowing component replacement and upgrade.

components.name: Material homogeneity
components.description: Use of mono-materials or easily separable material combinations.

components.name: Hazardous substance avoidance
components.description: Avoidance of substances that impede recycling (e.g., SVHC, halogenated flame retardants).

components.name: Recyclability labelling
components.description: Clear material identification and sorting instructions.

indicators.name: Recyclability Rate
indicators.formula: Mass of product components that can be recycled / Total product mass
indicators.description: Theoretical percentage of product mass that is recyclable given current recycling infrastructure and technology. Differs from actual recycling rate, which depends on collection and sorting.
indicators.assessmentMethod: Material-by-material assessment against local recycling capability

indicators.name: Recycled Content Percentage
indicators.formula: Mass of recycled material inputs / Total material input mass
indicators.description: Proportion of product mass derived from recycled sources (post-consumer and pre-consumer). Key metric under the EU Sustainable Products Regulation.

euRequirements.requirement: Minimum recycled content in plastic beverage bottles: 25%
Minimum recycled content in all plastic beverage bottles: 30%

indicators.name: Product Lifetime Extension
indicators.description: Metrics measuring actual or designed product lifetime relative to industry average, including warranty periods, maintenance support duration, and software update availability.

subMetrics.name: Average product lifetime
subMetrics.description: Mean time to end-of-life or replacement

subMetrics.name: Spare parts availability
subMetrics.description: Duration after sale for which spare parts are guaranteed available

subMetrics.name: Lifetime vs industry average
subMetrics.description: Product lifetime divided by industry average lifetime

euCircularEconomyMonitoringFramework.description: The EU Circular Economy Monitoring Framework tracks progress towards a circular economy across 11 indicators grouped into 4 themes. Established by COM(2018)29 and updated in 2023.

indicators.name: EU self-sufficiency for raw materials
indicators.description: Share of domestic production in domestic material consumption for critical raw materials.

indicators.name: Green public procurement
indicators.unit: % of public contracts with environmental criteria
indicators.description: Share of public procurement procedures that include environmental criteria.

indicators.name: Waste generation
indicators.unit: kg per capita and per GDP unit
indicators.description: Total waste generation (excluding major mineral wastes) per capita and per unit of GDP.

indicators.name: Food waste
indicators.description: Food waste generation per capita across the supply chain.
indicators.euTarget: Reduce food waste per capita by 30% by 2025 (retail and consumer) and 50% by 2030

indicators.name: Recycling rates
indicators.description: Recycling rate of municipal waste, all waste excluding major mineral waste, and specific waste streams (packaging, WEEE, bio-waste, construction and demolition).

indicators.name: Recycling of specific waste streams
indicators.description: Recycling rates for packaging waste (overall and by material: plastic, wood, ferrous metals, aluminium, glass, paper and cardboard), WEEE, bio-waste, and construction/demolition waste.

indicators.name: Contribution of recycled materials to raw materials demand (circular material use rate)
indicators.description: Share of material resources used that came from recycled waste materials (secondary raw materials) as a proportion of overall material use. EU average was approximately 11.7% in 2022.
indicators.euTarget: Double the circular material use rate by 2030 (from ~11% baseline)

indicators.name: Trade in recyclable raw materials
indicators.description: Trade flows (imports and exports) of recyclable raw materials between EU Member States and with third countries.

indicators.name: Private investment, jobs and gross value added related to circular economy sectors
indicators.description: Economic indicators for circular economy sectors including waste management, repair, reuse, and rental/leasing.

indicators.name: Patents related to recycling and secondary raw materials
indicators.description: Number of patents filed in recycling and secondary raw materials technologies.
